response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and platform engineer with 15+ years of software development, but he is a poor fit for this role. The two most critical requirements are native-level Japanese proficiency and strong C programming for embedded/UNIX development — he lists no Japanese and his C experience is marginal and dated (NI LabWindows in 2014–2015, a bachelor thesis project). His career is centered on web technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js) and distributed systems, with no demonstrated experience in embedded networking, LAN-switch firmware, security protocols such as MACsec or 802.1X, or Linux kernel modification. These gaps are fundamental to the role and cannot be bridged by his general engineering strengths.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, embedded, and distributed systems shows strong adaptability
Some exposure to Linux and low-level systems (embedded mention in bio, C/C++ at National Instruments, Linux-based audio server thesis)
Strong background in testable software practices, CI/CD, and code quality — valuable for any engineering team
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency at any level — a hard, non-negotiable requirement for this role
No experience with embedded networking security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, switch firmware) — the core technical domain of the position
C programming is minimal and dated (2014–2015); primary expertise is TypeScript/Node.js, which is largely irrelevant to embedded firmware development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
Broad engineering experience and Linux familiarity
